function.name: mcp_brave_search_brave_local_search
function.description:
Brave Local Search API provides enrichments for location search results. Access to this API is available only through the Brave Search API Pro plans; confirm the user's plan before using this tool (if the user does not have a Pro plan, use the brave_web_search tool). Searches for local businesses and places using Brave's Local Search API. Best for queries related to physical locations, businesses, restaurants, services, etc.
    
    Returns detailed information including:
        - Business names and addresses
        - Ratings and review counts
        - Phone numbers and opening hours

    Use this when the query implies 'near me', 'in my area', or mentions specific locations (e.g., 'in San Francisco'). This tool automatically falls back to brave_web_search if no local results are found.

country.description: Search query country, where the results come from. The country string is limited to 2 character country codes of supported countries.

search_lang.description: Search language preference. The 2 or more character language code for which the search results are provided.

ui_lang.description: The language of the UI. The 2 or more character language code for which the search results are provided.

count.description: Number of results (1-20, default 10). Applies only to web search results (i.e., has no effect on locations, news, videos, etc.)

safesearch.description: Filters search results for adult content. The following values are supported: 'off' - No filtering. 'moderate' - Filters explicit content (e.g., images and videos), but allows adult domains in search results. 'strict' - Drops all adult content from search results. The default value is 'moderate'.

freshness.description: Filters search results by when they were discovered. The following values are supported: 'pd' - Discovered within the last 24 hours. 'pw' - Discovered within the last 7 days. 'pm' - Discovered within the last 31 days. 'py' - Discovered within the last 365 days. 'YYYY-MM-DDtoYYYY-MM-DD' - Timeframe is also supported by specifying the date range e.g. 2022-04-01to2022-07-30.

text_decorations.description: Whether display strings (e.g. result snippets) should include decoration markers (e.g. highlighting characters).

spellcheck.description: Whether to spellcheck the provided query.

goggles.description: Goggles act as a custom re-ranking on top of Brave's search index. The parameter supports both a url where the Goggle is hosted or the definition of the Goggle. For more details, refer to the Goggles repository (i.e., https://github.com/brave/goggles-quickstart).

function.name: mcp_brave_search_brave_news_search
function.description:
This tool searches for news articles using Brave's News Search API based on the user's query. Use it when you need current news information, breaking news updates, or articles about specific topics, events, or entities.
    
    When to use:
        - Finding recent news articles on specific topics
        - Getting breaking news updates
        - Researching current events or trending stories
        - Gathering news sources and headlines for analysis

    Returns a JSON list of news-related results with title, url, and description. Some results may contain snippets of text from the article.
    
    When relaying results in markdown-supporting environments, always cite sources with hyperlinks.
    
    Examples:
        - "According to [Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/technology/china-bans/), China bans uncertified and recalled power banks on planes".
        - "The [New York Times](https://www.nytimes.com/2025/06/27/us/technology/ev-sales.html) reports that Tesla's EV sales have increased by 20%".
        - "According to [BBC News](https://www.bbc.com/news/world-europe-65910000), the UK government has announced a new policy to support renewable energy".

function.name: mcp_brave_search_brave_summarizer
function.description:
Retrieves AI-generated summaries of web search results using Brave's Summarizer API. This tool processes search results to create concise, coherent summaries of information gathered from multiple sources.

    When to use:

    - When you need a concise overview of complex topics from multiple sources
    - For quick fact-checking or getting key points without reading full articles
    - When providing users with summarized information that synthesizes various perspectives
    - For research tasks requiring distilled information from web searches

    Returns a text summary that consolidates information from the search results. Optional features include inline references to source URLs and additional entity information.

    Requirements: Must first perform a web search using brave_web_search with summary=true parameter. Requires a Pro AI subscription to access the summarizer functionality.

key.description: The key is equal to value of field key as part of the Summarizer response model.

entity_info.description: Returns extra entities info with the summary response.

inline_references.description: Adds inline references to the summary response.

function.name: mcp_context7_query_docs
function.description:
Retrieves and queries up-to-date documentation and code examples from Context7 for any programming library or framework.

You must call 'Resolve Context7 Library ID' tool first to obtain the exact Context7-compatible library ID required to use this tool, UNLESS the user explicitly provides a library ID in the format '/org/project' or '/org/project/version' in their query.

Do not call this tool more than 3 times per question.

libraryId.description: Exact Context7-compatible library ID (e.g., '/mongodb/docs', '/vercel/next.js', '/supabase/supabase', '/vercel/next.js/v14.3.0-canary.87') retrieved from 'resolve-library-id' or directly from user query in the format '/org/project' or '/org/project/version'.

query.description: The question or task you need help with. Be specific and include relevant details. Good: 'How to set up authentication with JWT in Express.js' or 'React useEffect cleanup function examples'. Bad: 'auth' or 'hooks'. The query is sent to the Context7 API for processing. Do not include any sensitive or confidential information such as API keys, passwords, credentials, personal data, or proprietary code in your query.

function.name: mcp_context7_resolve_library_id
function.description:
Resolves a package/product name to a Context7-compatible library ID and returns matching libraries.

You MUST call this function before 'Query Documentation' tool to obtain a valid Context7-compatible library ID UNLESS the user explicitly provides a library ID in the format '/org/project' or '/org/project/version' in their query.

Each result includes:
- Library ID: Context7-compatible identifier (format: /org/project)
- Name: Library or package name
- Description: Short summary
- Code Snippets: Number of available code examples
- Source Reputation: Authority indicator (High, Medium, Low, or Unknown)
- Benchmark Score: Quality indicator (100 is the highest score)
- Versions: List of versions if available. Use one of those versions if the user provides a version in their query. The format of the version is /org/project/version.

For best results, select libraries based on name match, source reputation, snippet coverage, benchmark score, and relevance to your use case.

Selection Process:
1. Analyze the query to understand what library/package the user is looking for
2. Return the most relevant match based on:
- Name similarity to the query (exact matches prioritized)
- Description relevance to the query's intent
- Documentation coverage (prioritize libraries with higher Code Snippet counts)
- Source reputation (consider libraries with High or Medium reputation more authoritative)
- Benchmark Score: Quality indicator (100 is the highest score)

Response Format:
- Return the selected library ID in a clearly marked section
- Provide a brief explanation for why this library was chosen
- If multiple good matches exist, acknowledge this but proceed with the most relevant one
- If no good matches exist, clearly state this and suggest query refinements

For ambiguous queries, request clarification before proceeding with a best-guess match.

IMPORTANT: Do not call this tool more than 3 times per question. If you cannot find what you need after 3 calls, use the best result you have.

query.description: The question or task you need help with. This is used to rank library results by relevance to what the user is trying to accomplish. The query is sent to the Context7 API for processing. Do not include any sensitive or confidential information such as API keys, passwords, credentials, personal data, or proprietary code in your query.

libraryName.description: Library name to search for and retrieve a Context7-compatible library ID. Use the official library name with proper punctuation — e.g., 'Next.js' instead of 'nextjs', 'Customer.io' instead of 'customerio', 'Three.js' instead of 'threejs'.

function.name: mcp_exa_web_search_exa

Search the web for any topic and get clean, ready-to-use content.

      Best for: Finding current information, news, facts, people, companies, or answering questions about any topic.

      Query tips: 
      describe the ideal page, not keywords. "blog post comparing React and Vue performance" not "React vs Vue".

function.name: mcp_sequential_thinking_sequentialthinking
function.description:
A detailed tool for dynamic and reflective problem-solving through thoughts.
This tool helps analyze problems through a flexible thinking process that can adapt and evolve.
Each thought can build on, question, or revise previous insights as understanding deepens.

When to use this tool:
- Breaking down complex problems into steps
- Planning and design with room for revision
- Analysis that might need course correction
- Problems where the full scope might not be clear initially
- Problems that require a multi-step solution
- Tasks that need to maintain context over multiple steps
- Situations where irrelevant information needs to be filtered out

Key features:
- You can adjust total_thoughts up or down as you progress
- You can question or revise previous thoughts
- You can add more thoughts even after reaching what seemed like the end
- You can express uncertainty and explore alternative approaches
- Not every thought needs to build linearly - you can branch or backtrack
- Generates a solution hypothesis
- Verifies the hypothesis based on the Chain of Thought steps
- Repeats the process until satisfied
- Provides a correct answer

Parameters explained:
- thought: Your current thinking step, which can include:
  * Regular analytical steps
  * Revisions of previous thoughts
  * Questions about previous decisions
  * Realizations about needing more analysis
  * Changes in approach
  * Hypothesis generation
  * Hypothesis verification
- nextThoughtNeeded: True if you need more thinking, even if at what seemed like the end
- thoughtNumber: Current number in sequence (can go beyond initial total if needed)
- totalThoughts: Current estimate of thoughts needed (can be adjusted up/down)
- isRevision: A boolean indicating if this thought revises previous thinking
- revisesThought: If is_revision is true, which thought number is being reconsidered
- branchFromThought: If branching, which thought number is the branching point
- branchId: Identifier for the current branch (if any)
- needsMoreThoughts: If reaching end but realizing more thoughts needed

You should:
1. Start with an initial estimate of needed thoughts, but be ready to adjust
2. Feel free to question or revise previous thoughts
3. Don't hesitate to add more thoughts if needed, even at the "end"
4. Express uncertainty when present
5. Mark thoughts that revise previous thinking or branch into new paths
6. Ignore information that is irrelevant to the current step
7. Generate a solution hypothesis when appropriate
8. Verify the hypothesis based on the Chain of Thought steps
9. Repeat the process until satisfied with the solution
10. Provide a single, ideally correct answer as the final output
11. Only set nextThoughtNeeded to false when truly done and a satisfactory answer is reached

nextThoughtNeeded.description: Whether another thought step is needed

totalThoughts.description: Estimated total thoughts needed (numeric value, e.g., 5, 10)

function.name: patch
function.description:
Targeted find-and-replace edits in files. Use this instead of sed/awk in terminal. Uses fuzzy matching (9 strategies) so minor whitespace/indentation differences won't break it. Returns a unified diff. Auto-runs syntax checks after editing.

Replace mode (default): find a unique string and replace it.
Patch mode: apply V4A multi-file patches for bulk changes.

mode.description: Edit mode: 'replace' for targeted find-and-replace, 'patch' for V4A multi-file patches

path.description: File path to edit (required for 'replace' mode)

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'replace' mode). Must be unique in the file unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'replace' mode). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: Replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false)

patch.description:
V4A format patch content (required for 'patch' mode). Format:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: path/to/file
@@ context hint @@
 context line
-removed line
+added line
*** End Patch

function.name: read_file
function.description: Read a text file with line numbers and pagination. Use this instead of cat/head/tail in terminal. Output format: 'LINE_NUM|CONTENT'. Suggests similar filenames if not found. Use offset and limit for large files. Reads exceeding ~100K characters are rejected; use offset and limit to read specific sections of large files. NOTE: Cannot read images or binary files — use vision_analyze for images.

path.description: Path to the file to read (absolute, relative, or ~/path)

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed, default: 1)

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read (default: 500, max: 2000)

function.name: search_files
function.description:
Search file contents or find files by name. Use this instead of grep/rg/find/ls in terminal. Ripgrep-backed, faster than shell equivalents.

Content search (target='content'): Regex search inside files. Output modes: full matches with line numbers, file paths only, or match counts.

File search (target='files'): Find files by glob pattern (e.g., '*.py', '*config*'). Also use this instead of ls — results sorted by modification time.

pattern.description: Regex pattern for content search, or glob pattern (e.g., '*.py') for file search

target.description: 'content' searches inside file contents, 'files' searches for files by name

path.description: Directory or file to search in (default: current working directory)

file_glob.description: Filter files by pattern in grep mode (e.g., '*.py' to only search Python files)

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 50)

offset.description: Skip first N results for pagination (default: 0)

output_mode.description: Output format for grep mode: 'content' shows matching lines with line numbers, 'files_only' lists file paths, 'count' shows match counts per file

context.description: Number of context lines before and after each match (grep mode only)

function.name: write_file
function.description: Write content to a file, completely replacing existing content. Use this instead of echo/cat heredoc in terminal. Creates parent directories automatically. OVERWRITES the entire file — use 'patch' for targeted edits. Auto-runs syntax checks on .py/.json/.yaml/.toml and other linted languages; only NEW errors introduced by this write are surfaced (pre-existing errors are filtered out).

path.description: Path to the file to write (will be created if it doesn't exist, overwritten if it does)

content.description: Complete content to write to the file

Create Batch 6 — 10 articles. Remaining topics.

1. blog/en/dac8-crypto-reporting-2026-strategy.mdx
   "DAC8 Crypto Reporting: What Changes January 2026 and What to Do About It" — Frontline article on EU crypto reporting. Impact on holders in NL (36% unrealised), DE (26.375% short), ES (28%), IT (33% 2026). PY as solution.

2. blog/en/crypto-countries-0-capital-gains-tax.mdx
   "7 Countries With 0% Crypto Capital Gains Tax (2026)" — Listicle. PY, UAE, Qatar, Cayman, BVI, Bahamas, Bermuda. Compare on: residency path, citizenship, cost, CRS. PY wins overall.

3. blog/en/crypto-friendly-banking-paraguay.mdx
   "Crypto-Friendly Banking in Paraguay: X4T, Ueno, Oobit & More" — X4T: licensed PY exchange, bank-integrated. Ueno: crypto-friendly digital bank. Oobit: crypto spending. Banks accepting crypto: Schwab International, Itaú (selective).

4. blog/en/crypto-tax-exiles-paraguay.mdx
   "Crypto Tax Exiles: Why European Investors Are Choosing Paraguay in 2026" — European crypto holders: 49M+. NL investors face 36% unrealised. DE 26.375% under 1yr. PY 0%. Case studies.

5. blog/en/paraguay-residency-for-crypto-investors.mdx
   "Paraguay Residency for Crypto Investors: Complete Guide" — Process, costs ($2,900+), timeline, tax treatment. DNIT Res 47/2026 explained (reporting ≠ tax).

6. blog/en/dnit-resolution-47-paraguay-crypto.mdx
   "DNIT Resolution 47/2026: What Crypto Investors Must Report (And Why It's Not a Tax)" — Explain the resolution. It's AML/KYC reporting, not a crypto tax. No capital gains, no wealth tax. Compare to DAC8 in EU.

7. blog/en/paraguay-vs-portugal-crypto-tax.mdx
   "Paraguay vs Portugal for Crypto Investors: 2026 Tax Showdown" — Portugal NHR dead. Crypto gains now up to 48% tax in PT. PY 0%. Plus no CRS in PY, citizenship in 3 vs 5 years.

8. blog/en/australia-tax-paraguay-alternative.mdx
   "Australia's 47% Top Tax Rate vs Paraguay's 0%: The Numbers" — AU tax breakdown: 47% top, 30% corporate, CGT discount 50%. PY: 0% foreign, 10% corporate. Concrete comparison.

9. blog/en/south-africa-paraguay-tax-strategy.mdx
   "South Africa to Paraguay Tax Strategy: From 45% to 0%" — SA: 45% top rate, no wealth/inheritance tax but CGT inclusion. PY: 0% foreign, territorial. Document complexity creates moat.

10. blog/nl/paraguay-stappenplan-residency.mdx
    "Stappenplan: Van Box 3 naar Paraguay in 6 Maanden" — NL step-by-step: month 1 document gathering, month 2 first trip, month 3-4 processing, month 5 second trip, month 6 cédula. Concrete timeline with costs per step.

Write in target languages. Frontmatter with all required fields.
I'll research current facts first, then create all 10 articles.

{"content": "     1|---
     2|title: "7 Countries With 0% Foreign Income Tax in 2026 (And How They Compare)"
     3|slug: "seven-countries-zero-foreign-income-tax"
     4|date: "2026-05-07"

     8|coverImage: "/images/blog/default-cover.webp"

    10|draft: false

    12|
    13|## The Zero-Tax Club: 7 Countries Compared
    14|
    15|If you want to legally pay 0% tax on your foreign income, you have options. But they're not all created equal. Some require massive investments, others offer no path to citizenship, and some have cost of living that eats up your savings.
    16|
    17|Here's the definitive comparison of all seven countries with 0% foreign income tax in 2026.
    18|
    19|## The Quick Comparison
    20|
    21|| Factor | Paraguay | Panama | UAE | Costa Rica | Qatar | Oman | Bahrain |
    22||--------|----------|--------|-----|-----------|------|------|---------|
    23|| **Foreign Income Tax** | 0% | 0% | 0% | 0% | 0% | 0% | 0% |
    24|| **Local Income Tax** | 10% (PY-sourced) | Up to 25% | 0% | 15-25% | 0% | 0% | 0% |
    25|| **VAT/Sales Tax** | 10% | 7% | 5% | 13% | 0% | 5% | 5% |
    26|| **Wealth Tax** | 0% | 0% | 0% | 0% | 0% | 0% | 0% |
    27|| **Capital Gains Tax** | 0% (foreign) | 0% (foreign) | 0% | 0% (foreign) | 0% | 0% | 0% |

title: "DAC8 Crypto Reporting: What Changes January 2026 and What to Do About It"
slug: "dac8-crypto-reporting-2026-strategy"
date: "2026-05-08"
author: "Nexa Paraguay"

## DAC8 Is Live: What Crypto Investors Need to Know

On January 1, 2026, the European Union's DAC8 directive went into full effect. This is the most significant regulatory shift for crypto holders in EU history. Here's what changed, who it affects, and what you can do about it.

## What Is DAC8?

DAC8 (Directive on Administrative Cooperation, 8th iteration) requires all crypto-asset service providers — exchanges, brokers, wallet providers — operating in the EU to:

- **Collect detailed user data** including identity, residence, and transaction history
- **Report all crypto transactions** to national tax authorities automatically
- **Share data across EU member states** via automatic exchange of information

The first reporting deadline is September 2027 for the 2026 calendar year. But the data collection starts now.

## Country-by-Country Impact on Crypto Holders

### Netherlands: 36% Tax on Unrealised Gains

The Netherlands has taken the most aggressive stance. Under the reformed Box 3 system:

- **36% flat tax** on deemed returns from investments, including crypto
- **Tax applies even if you haven't sold** — unrealised gains are taxed
- **€57,000 exemption** per individual (€114,000 per couple)
- **Effective from 2026**, fully phased in by 2028

For a Dutch investor holding €500,000 in crypto that appreciated by 20% (€100,000), the tax bill is €36,000 — even if they never sold a single coin.

### Germany: 26.375% Short-Term (Plus Soli)

Germany's crypto tax regime:

- **Short-term gains** (held <1 year): taxed at your personal income tax rate, up to 45% plus 5.5% solidarity surcharge = **up to 47.475%**
- **The 1-year holding exemption is under threat** — as of April 2026, legislative changes are being debated to abolish it entirely
- **€600 annual exemption** for private sales
- **Cryptocurrency is classified as private assets**, not capital assets

A German trader making €100,000 in short-term crypto gains at the top bracket pays approximately €47,475 in tax.

### Spain: Up to 28% Capital Gains

Spain taxes crypto gains as savings income:

- **19% up to €6,000**
- **21% from €6,001 to €50,000**
- **23% from €50,001 to €200,000**
- **27% from €200,001 to €300,000**
- **28% over €300,000**

A Spanish investor with €500,000 in crypto gains faces €134,000+ in tax.

### Italy: 33% Flat Rate (Up from 26%)

Italy raised its crypto capital gains tax:

- **33% flat rate** from January 1, 2026 (up from 26%)
- **€2,000 exemption abolished** — all gains are now taxable
- **No distinction** between short-term and long-term holding

An Italian investor with €200,000 in crypto gains pays €66,000 in tax.

## The EU's Enforcement Arsenal

DAC8 isn't just a reporting requirement — it's an enforcement mechanism:

- **Automatic data exchange** between all 27 EU member states
- **Wallet-level reporting** for transactions over €1,000
- **DeFi and self-custody wallets NOT yet covered**, but the EU has signaled this is coming
- **Penalties for non-compliance** up to €5 million or 5% of annual turnover for platforms

## The Paraguay Solution: 0% on Foreign Crypto

While EU countries are racing to tax crypto gains more efficiently, Paraguay maintains a fundamentally different approach.

### Paraguay's Crypto Tax Treatment

| Aspect | Paraguay |
|--------|----------|
| **Foreign crypto gains** | 0% tax |
| **Capital gains tax** | 0% (foreign-source) |
| **Wealth tax** | 0% |
| **Inheritance tax** | 0% |
| **CRS reporting** | Not a participant |
| **DAC8** | Not applicable |
| **DNIT Res 47/2026** | Reporting only, not a tax |

### Why This Works

Paraguay operates a territorial tax system. Income sourced outside Paraguay — including crypto trades on foreign exchanges, DeFi yields, and NFT sales — is taxed at 0%. DNIT Resolution 47/2026 introduced reporting obligations in March 2026, but these are AML/KYC requirements, not a crypto tax. No capital gains tax, no wealth tax, no exit tax.

### The Numbers Don't Lie

Compare a €1,000,000 crypto portfolio across jurisdictions:

| Jurisdiction | Tax on €200k Gain | Effective Rate |
|-------------|------------------|----------------|
| Netherlands (Box 3) | €72,000 | 36% |
| Germany (short-term, top bracket) | ~€95,000 | ~47% |
| Spain (top bracket) | ~€56,000 | 28% |
| Italy (flat 33%) | €66,000 | 33% |
| **Paraguay** | **€0** | **0%** |

## What to Do If You're an EU Crypto Holder

### 1. Assess Your Exposure

Calculate what you'd owe under DAC8 reporting. Remember: tax authorities will have your data automatically starting with 2026 transactions.

### 2. Consider Relocation

Paraguay offers a clear path:

- **Permanent residency** in 3-6 months (traditional route, $2,900+)
- **Investor Pass** immediate permanent residency from $150,000 investment
- **Citizenship** after 3 years of residency
- **No minimum physical presence** required (visit every 3 years)

### 3. Plan Your Exit

EU exit tax rules vary by country. Some apply exit taxes on unrealised gains when you leave. Plan with a qualified advisor.

### 4. Use the Right Infrastructure

Once in Paraguay, you have access to:

- **X4T** — Paraguay's first licensed crypto exchange, bank-integrated
- **Ueno** — crypto-friendly digital bank
- **Oobit** — crypto spending card accepted locally and worldwide

## The Bottom Line

DAC8 is the beginning, not the end. With the EU pushing for wallet-level reporting, DeFi regulation, and higher crypto tax rates, the window for European crypto holders to restructure their tax residence is narrowing. Paraguay offers a proven, legal alternative with 0% tax on foreign crypto gains, no CRS reporting, and a fast path to a second passport.

The question isn't whether DAC8 will impact you. It's whether you'll act before the 2026 data starts flowing to your local tax authority.

In Paraguay, the answer is increasingly yes — as long as you know the right platforms.

## X4T: Paraguay's First Licensed Crypto Exchange

X4T launched in December 2021 as Paraguay's first government-licensed cryptocurrency exchange. It has since become the largest and most trusted platform in the country.

### What Makes X4T Different

**Licensed and Regulated**
X4T operates under full regulatory oversight in Paraguay. Unlike offshore exchanges that operate in grey areas, X4T is fully compliant with local law, including DNIT Resolution 47/2026 reporting requirements. This means:

- Your funds are held by a regulated entity
- Bank integrations are legally structured
- No sudden "unsupported jurisdiction" messages
- Full audit trail for your tax filings

**Bank Integration**
X4T allows you to buy and sell crypto directly via bank transfer to Paraguayan bank accounts (Ueno, Itaú, and others). You can:

- Deposit guaranis via bank transfer
- Buy USDT, BTC, ETH, and major altcoins
- Sell back to fiat and withdraw to your local bank account
- Use the mobile app for on-the-go trading

**Zero Trading Fees**
X4T charges 0% trading fees — significantly cheaper than Binance, Kraken, or Coinbase.

**Dollar Accounts**
X4T offers dollar-denominated account options, allowing you to hold USD alongside your crypto, reducing FX exposure.

### How to Get Started with X4T

1. Download the app (iOS and Android)

- **US dollar accounts** — hold USD alongside PYG
- **Debit card** — spend from your account at any POS terminal
- **Mobile-first** — everything happens through the app

### Why Ueno Works for Crypto Investors

Ueno understands that crypto investors need to move between fiat and digital assets. Their onboarding process is straightforward, and they don't freeze accounts for legitimate exchange activity. However, you should still be prepared to explain your source of funds with proper documentation.

- Works with Paraguay's POS network
- Supports USDT, BTC, ETH, and major stablecoins
- No need to sell crypto to fiat first
- Instant conversion at market rates

Charles Schwab International accounts are available to Paraguay residents. Schwab is among the most crypto-friendly traditional brokerages:

- No foreign transaction fees
- Unlimited ATM fee rebates globally
- Access to US stock market, bonds, ETFs
- No issue with transfers from crypto exchanges (within reason)

**Note:** Schwab is a US brokerage. Your accounts may be reportable under FATCA (US accounts held by foreign residents). But Schwab does not participate in CRS for non-US persons.

Itaú is one of Brazil's largest banks, and its Paraguayan branch offers some flexibility for crypto-adjacent clients. However, Itaú is more selective than Ueno or Schwab.

The key to successful banking in Paraguay as a crypto investor is:

## The European Tax Crisis for Crypto Holders

### Netherlands: The Unrealised Gains Trap

The Netherlands has implemented one of the most aggressive tax regimes in the world for crypto investors.

**Pre-2026:** Box 3 taxed deemed returns on a fictional 5.88% yield at 36% — whether you earned anything or not.

**2026 and beyond:** The new system taxes actual returns, including unrealised price appreciation, at 36%. If your Bitcoin goes up in value but you don't sell, you still owe 36% of that increase.

### Germany: The End of the 1-Year Rule

Germany historically offered one of the best crypto tax regimes in Europe: hold for 1 year and sell tax-free. This is now under active threat.

**Current rules:**
- Short-term gains (<1 year): taxed at personal income tax rate up to 45% + 5.5% solidarity surcharge = up to 47.475%

### Spain: 28% on Large Gains

Spain taxes crypto gains as savings income with progressive rates reaching 28% on gains over €300,000.

**The problem:** Spain's tax authorities (Agencia Tributaria) have become increasingly aggressive on crypto enforcement. DAC8 will give them full visibility into Spanish residents' foreign exchange accounts.

I rent a beautiful apartment in Asuncion for €600/month and live like a king.*" — Carlos, former Spanish resident

Italy raised its crypto capital gains tax from 26% to 33% effective January 1, 2026, and abolished the €2,000 exemption threshold.

**The math:** An Italian investor with €500,000 in crypto gains now owes €165,000 in tax. Years of crypto profits built through careful investing, taxed at one-third.

"*The 33% rate was the final straw. I had been thinking about leaving for years, but when they raised the rate and removed the exemption, I realized Italy would never be friendly to crypto wealth. I now live in Paraguay, pay 0% on my foreign crypto income, and I'm on track for citizenship in 3 years.*" — Marco, former Italian resident

## Paraguay Residency for Crypto Investors: The Essential Guide

**Timeline:** 2-4 months

For most crypto investors, the traditional temporary residency route is the best option. It costs $2,900+, takes about the same time, and leads to citizenship after 3 years of temporary residency.

The Investor Pass makes sense if you want permanent residency immediately and have the capital to deploy in Paraguayan real estate or business.

## DNIT Resolution 47/2026: The Full Breakdown

In March 2026, Paraguay's National Directorate of Tax Revenue (DNIT) issued General Resolution No. 47/2026, introducing comprehensive reporting requirements for cryptocurrency transactions.

Headlines around the world screamed: "Paraguay Tightens Crypto Rules" and "New Crypto Tax in Paraguay."

The reality is far more nuanced — and far more favorable to crypto investors than the headlines suggest.

## What DNIT Resolution 47/2026 Actually Says

### The Legal Framework

Resolution 47/2026 is issued under the authority of Paraguay's tax code (Ley 125/91 and its modifications) and aligns with Financial Action Task Force (FATF) Recommendation 15, which addresses virtual assets and virtual asset service providers.

**Key provisions:**

1. **Reporting obligation for individuals:** Any resident individual conducting crypto transactions exceeding $5,000 in aggregate annual value must file a declaration.

2. **Reporting obligation for platforms:** All crypto-asset service providers operating in Paraguay must report user transaction data.

3. **What must be reported:**
   - Total annual transaction volume
   - Wallet addresses used
   - Counterparty identification (where available)
   - Type of crypto assets transacted
   - Date ranges of activity

4. **Timing:** Annual filing, due within 90 days after the close of the fiscal year.

### What Is NOT in the Resolution

Crucially, Resolution 47/2026 does NOT:

- **Impose a capital gains tax on crypto** — no mention of taxing gains
- **Create a wealth tax on crypto holdings** — no tax on total portfolio value
- **Change the territorial tax system** — foreign income remains untaxed
- **Introduce an exit tax** — leaving Paraguay has no tax consequence
- **Require reporting of every transaction** — only aggregated annual data
- **Apply retroactively** — only forward-looking from March 2026

## Why It's Not a Crypto Tax

This is the single most important distinction to understand.

### What a Tax Looks Like (Examples)

| Country | Crypto Tax | Rate |
|---------|-----------|------|
| Italy | Capital gains tax | 33% flat |
| Netherlands | Unrealised gains tax | 36% |
| Germany | Short-term income tax | Up to 47.5% |
| Spain | Savings income tax | Up to 28% |
| Australia | Capital gains tax | Up to 47% (with CGT discount) |

### What Paraguay Has

| Activity | Tax Rate |
|----------|---------|
| Foreign crypto trading gains | 0% |
| Foreign crypto staking yield | 0% |
| Foreign DeFi income | 0% |
| Foreign NFT sales | 0% |
| Crypto held as investment | 0% wealth tax |
| Crypto passed to heirs | 0% inheritance tax |
| **Annual reporting compliance** | **Required** |

The reporting requirement exists to combat money laundering and terrorist financing, not to collect tax revenue. Paraguay's territorial tax system has not changed. Foreign-source income — including crypto gains — remains at 0%.

## DNIT Res 47 vs. EU DAC8: A Critical Comparison

Both are reporting frameworks. But the end result is completely different.

| Aspect | EU DAC8 | Paraguay DNIT 47 |
|--------|---------|-------------------|
| **Effective date** | January 1, 2026 | March 2026 |
| **Reporting required** | Yes | Yes |
| **Transaction threshold** | €1,000+ | $5,000+ annual |
| **Wallet-level reporting** | Planned | Yes |
| **DeFi covered** | Not yet | Covered |
| **Data shared internationally** | Yes, all EU states | No (not CRS-compatible) |
| **Tax rate on crypto gains** | 28%-48% depending on country | 0% |
| **Tax on unrealised gains** | In Netherlands only (36%) | 0% |
| **Penalties for non-reporting** | Yes | Yes (administrative) |
| **Purpose** | Tax enforcement | AML/KYC compliance |

The critical difference: DAC8's purpose is tax collection. It enables EU tax authorities to assess and collect crypto gains taxes. DNIT Res 47's purpose is transparency. Paraguay already has 0% tax on foreign crypto gains — there's nothing to collect.

## What This Means for Crypto Investors

### The Compliance Burden

You must file an annual declaration. But compared to tax filings in most countries, the burden is light:

- Declare aggregate annual transaction volume
- List wallet addresses
- No need to calculate gains or losses
- No tax payment required
- File through a local accountant (~$200-$500/year)

### What You Should NOT Do

1. **Don't panic** — this is not a tax
2. **Don't hide** — reporting is mandatory; non-compliance carries penalties
3. **Don't assume** — if you have questions about your specific situation, consult a Paraguayan tax attorney

### What You Should Do

1. Maintain records of all crypto transactions
2. Keep exchange statements and wallet logs
3. Hire a local accountant for annual filing
4. Continue operating normally — your 0% tax rate hasn't changed

## The Strategic Advantage

DNIT Res 47/2026 actually creates a competitive advantage for Paraguay over other jurisdictions:

In the EU, DAC8 reporting leads directly to tax bills. In Paraguay, reporting leads to... nothing. No tax due. This makes Paraguay the only jurisdiction where you can be fully compliant and pay 0%.

### Compared to Offshore Islands

The Cayman Islands, BVI, and Bahamas don't report either — but they also don't offer affordable entry, real citizenship paths, or the quality of life you can get in Paraguay for $1,200/month.

**"DNIT Res 47 means Paraguay is taxing crypto."**
False. It is a reporting resolution. No tax has been imposed on foreign crypto gains. The territorial tax system is unchanged.

**"I need to report every trade I make."**
No. Reporting is aggregated annual data, not per-transaction. You declare total volume, wallets, and crypto types.

**"My data will be shared with my home country."**
No. Paraguay does not participate in CRS. Your DNIT declaration stays in Paraguay.

**"Paraguay is becoming less crypto-friendly."**
False. Paraguay is becoming more regulated, not less friendly. Regulated environments attract legitimate business and banking relationships.

| **Effective Top Rate** | **47%** |

### Crypto Capital Gains Tax

In Australia, cryptocurrency is treated as a CGT asset by the ATO.

**Key rules:**
- Every crypto disposal triggers a CGT event
- This includes crypto-to-crypto trades (e.g., BTC to ETH)
- Airdrops, hard forks, and staking rewards are treated as income
- NFTs are subject to CGT on disposal

**The 50% CGT Discount:**
If you hold crypto for more than 12 months before disposing, you're eligible for a 50% CGT discount. Your taxable gain is halved.

| Scenario | Gain | After 50% Discount | Tax at 47% |
|----------|------|-------------------|------------|
| Short-term (buy/sell in 6 months) | $500,000 | N/A | $235,000 |
| Long-term (hold 3 years) | $500,000 | $250,000 | $117,500 |

Even with the 50% discount, a long-term holder still pays $117,500 on a $500,000 gain.

### Corporate Tax

- Base rate entity (turnover <$50M): 25%
- All other companies: 30%

### Other Taxes

- **GST:** 10% (standard rate)
- **Stamp duty:** Varies by state (up to 7%)
- **Land tax:** Varies by state
- **No inheritance tax** (federal estate duty abolished)
- **No wealth tax**

## Paraguay's Tax System: The Full Breakdown

### Personal Income Tax

| Income Type | Paraguay Rate |
|-------------|--------------|
| Foreign-source income (including crypto) | 0% |
| Paraguayan-source employment income | 10% |
| Paraguayan-source business income | 10% |

### Crypto Capital Gains

| Scenario | Paraguay Rate |
|----------|-------------|
| Foreign crypto trading (on Binance, Coinbase, etc.) | 0% |
| Foreign crypto staking/yield | 0% |
| Foreign DeFi activities | 0% |
| Foreign NFT sales | 0% |
| Paraguayan crypto trading (X4T, local platforms) | 10% (if PY-sourced) |

### Corporate Tax

- Standard corporate rate: 10%
- Small businesses: 10%

### Other Taxes

- **VAT (IVA):** 10% (standard), 5% (some services)
- **Wealth tax:** 0%
- **Inheritance tax:** 0%
- **Property tax:** 0.5%-1% of assessed value
- **Exit tax:** 0%

## The Numbers: Concrete Comparison

### Scenario 1: High-Income Crypto Trader

**Profile:** $1M AUD in crypto, active trader, $300K annual gains

| Tax Item | Australia | Paraguay |
|----------|-----------|----------|
| Crypto trading gains (short-term) | $300,000 x 47% = **$141,000** | **$0** |
| Employment income (if earning $150K AU) | $48,667 | $0 (foreign-source) |
| Medicare Levy | $3,000 | $0 |
| **Total Tax** | **~$192,667** | **$0** |
| **After-tax income** | **$257,333** | **$450,000** |

### Scenario 2: Long-Term Holder Selling

**Profile:** $2M AUD crypto portfolio, held 3+ years, $800K gain

| Tax Item | Australia | Paraguay |
|----------|-----------|----------|
| Gross gain | $800,000 | $800,000 |
| 50% CGT discount applied | $400,000 | N/A |
| Taxable gain | $400,000 | $0 |
| Tax at 47% | **$188,000** | **$0** |
| **Net proceeds** | **$612,000** | **$800,000** |

### Scenario 3: Crypto Business Owner

**Profile:** Running a crypto-related business, $500K profit

| Tax Item | Australia | Paraguay |
|----------|-----------|----------|
| Corporate tax | $500,000 x 25% (base) = **$125,000** | $500,000 x 10% = **$50,000** |
| Dividend to shareholder | $375,000 | $450,000 |
| Tax on dividend (47% top) | $176,250 | $0 (foreign-source) |
| **Total tax burden** | **~$301,250** | **~$50,000** |
| **Effective rate** | **~60%** | **10%** |

## Beyond Tax: What Else Changes?

### CRS and ATO Visibility

**Australia:** CRS participating country. The ATO receives automatic data from financial institutions in 100+ countries. The ATO also has a dedicated Crypto Task Force targeting non-compliant investors.

**Paraguay:** Not a CRS participant. Your financial data stays in Paraguay. No automatic sharing with the ATO or any other tax authority.

### Cost of Living

| Expense | Sydney | Asuncion |
|---------|--------|----------|
| 2BR apartment (city center) | $3,500-$5,500 AUD/month | $700-$1,300 AUD/month |
| Groceries (monthly) | $800-$1,200 | $400-$700 |
| Dining out (meal for two) | $100-$200 | $30-$60 |
| Internet (100Mbps) | $80-$120/month | $45-$70/month |
| **Total comfortable living** | **$5,000-$7,500/month** | **$1,800-$3,000/month** |

### Citizenship

**Australia:** Citizenship after 4 years of permanent residency. Must pass citizenship test. No dual citizenship restrictions (Australia allows it).

**Paraguay:** Citizenship after 3 years of temporary residency. Basic Spanish required. Dual citizenship allowed. No continuous presence requirement.

### Quality of Life Factors

| Factor | Australia | Paraguay |
|--------|-----------|----------|
| Safety | Very safe | Safe (urban precautions) |
| Healthcare | Medicare (public) | Private insurance ($50-100/mo) |
| Education | Excellent | Good (international schools) |
| Infrastructure | Excellent | Good (improving) |
| English proficiency | Native | Limited (Spanish) |
| Time zone | AEST (UTC+10) | PYT (UTC-4) |
| Visa-free travel | 185+ countries | ~130 countries |

## The Exit Strategy: Moving from Australia to Paraguay

### Step 1: Cease Australian Tax Residency

The ATO has strict rules for ceasing residency:

- Intend to leave permanently
- Establish permanent home in Paraguay
- Spend less than 183 days/year in Australia (preferably 0)
- Terminate Australian Medicare, bank accounts, driver's license
- File final Australian tax return

### Step 2: Obtain Paraguay Residency

- Start document preparation (1 month)
- Submit application (1-2 months processing)
- First trip to Paraguay (1 week)

- **Total: 3-6 months**

### Step 3: Transfer Crypto Holdings

- Sell or transfer crypto to your new Paraguayan accounts
- No Australian CGT on disposal after residency ceases (with proper exit planning)
- No Paraguay CGT on foreign crypto

### Step 4: Settle In

- Open Ueno bank account
- Register with X4T exchange
- Set up Oobit for crypto spending
- File DNIT Res 47 annual report

## The Bottom Line

Australia offers a high quality of life, but its tax system is punishing for crypto investors. The 47% top rate (including Medicare), CGT on every crypto disposal, and aggressive ATO enforcement make serious crypto wealth-building difficult.

Paraguay offers 0% tax on foreign crypto gains, a 10% corporate rate, no CRS, and citizenship in 3 years. For an Australian crypto investor with significant holdings, the math is overwhelming: tens or hundreds of thousands of dollars saved annually, a faster path to a second passport, and a dramatically lower cost of living.

The ATO's crypto surveillance is only getting better. DAC8 and CARF will only increase data sharing. The best time to restructure your tax residency was 2024. The second best time is now.

{"content": "---
title: "South Africa to Paraguay Tax Strategy: From 45% to 0%"
slug: "south-africa-paraguay-tax-strategy"
date: "2026-05-08"
author: "Nexa Paraguay"
category: "Tax"
excerpt: "South Africa taxes crypto gains up to 45% (income) or 18% effective (CGT). Paraguay taxes foreign crypto at 0%. Plus: SA's document complexity creates a natural moat for those who navigate it. Complete comparison and step-by-step strategy for South African crypto investors."
coverImage: "/images/blog/default-cover.webp"
readingMinutes: 8
draft: false
---

## From SA's 45% to Paraguay's 0%

South Africa's crypto tax landscape is complex. Whether you're classified as an investor (CGT) or a trader (income), the tax burden is significant. And with SARS increasingly focused on crypto enforcement through the CARF framework and enhanced data collection, South African crypto holders need a strategy.

Paraguay offers a clear path to 0% tax on foreign crypto gains. Here's how to make the transition.

## South Africa's Crypto Tax System (2026)

### The Classification Problem

In South Africa, crypto tax treatment depends on your classification:

**Capital vs. Revenue: The Critical Distinction**

| Classification | Crypto Activity | Tax Rate |
|---------------|----------------|----------|
| Capital asset (investor) | Long-term holding, infrequent trades | CGT — effective max 18% |
| Revenue (trader) | Frequent trading, business-like activity | Income tax — up to 45% |

SARS determines your classification based on:
- Frequency of transactions
- Intent at time of acquisition
- Period of ownership
- Nature of your profession
- Whether you have a separate trading business

### CGT for Investors

If classified as a capital asset:

- Inclusion rate for individuals: 40% (of gain included in income)
- Maximum marginal rate: 45%
- **Effective maximum CGT rate: 18%** (40% x 45%)
- Annual exclusion: R40,000 of capital gains (R2,000 for deceased estates)
- Primary residence exclusion: R2 million

**Example:** R1,000,000 crypto gain as investor
- Inclusion: R1,000,000 x 40% = R400,000
- Tax at 45% top bracket: R400,000 x 45% = R180,000
- Effective rate: 18%

### Income Tax for Traders

If classified as trading income:

- Marginal rates from 18% to 45%
- No CGT discount
- Every trade is a taxable event
- **Effective rate: up to 45%**

**Example:** R1,000,000 crypto gain as trader
- Full amount taxed at marginal rate
- Tax at 45%: R450,000
- Effective rate: 45%

### Other South African Tax Considerations

| Tax Type | Rate |
|----------|------|
| Corporate tax | 27% |
| VAT | 15% |
| Capital gains inclusion | 40% (individuals), 80% (companies) |
| Dividends withholding tax | 20% |
| Wealth tax | None |
| Inheritance tax | 20% (first R30M), 25% (above) |
| Donations tax | 20% (first R30M), 25% (above) |

## Paraguay's Crypto Tax System

| Aspect | Paraguay |
|--------|----------|
| Foreign crypto gains | 0% |
| Local crypto gains (PY-source) | 10% |
| Wealth tax on crypto | 0% |
| Inheritance tax | 0% |
| Corporate tax | 10% |
| VAT (IVA) | 10% |
| CRS participation | No |
| Reporting requirement | DNIT Res 47/2026 (AML only) |

## The Document Challenge: Why SA's Complexity Creates a Moat

One factor that makes Paraguay particularly valuable for South Africans: the complexity of South Africa's tax and financial documentation system.

### What South African Crypto Investors Need to Prepare

To leave South Africa's tax net and establish Paraguayan residency, you need:

1. **SARS tax clearance** — proving you're up to date with filings
2. **Capital gains history** — documenting cost basis for all crypto
3. **Exchange records** — history from Binance, Luno, VALR, etc.
4. **Bank statements** — proving fiat movements
5. **FIA approval** (if applicable) — exchange control approval for large transfers
6. **Will and estate planning documents** — SA has complex estate rules

### Why This Is a Moat

The document burden keeps most people from acting. They look at the requirements and delay. And delay. And delay.

But for those who navigate the complexity:

- **Less competition** for Paraguayan residency slots
- **Higher quality applicants** — those who get through are serious
- **More established infrastructure** for SA-PY transfers
- **Growing community** of South African expats in Paraguay

The document complexity filters out the unserious. If you can manage the paperwork, you're among the minority who will benefit from Paraguay's 0% regime before it becomes mainstream.

## Step-by-Step Strategy: From South Africa to Paraguay

### Phase 1: Preparation (Month 1-2)

1. **Gather documents:**
   - Certified birth certificate (apostille)
   - Police clearance from SAPS (apostille)
   - SARS tax clearance certificate
   - Bank reference letters
   - Crypto exchange statements (comprehensive history)

2. **Organize your crypto records:**
   - Transaction history from all exchanges
   - Wallet addresses and transaction logs
   - Cost basis calculations
   - Note: this complexity is exactly why most won't do it

3. **Consult professionals:**
   - South African tax advisor (for exit tax planning)
   - Paraguayan immigration lawyer
   - Forex specialist (for cross-border transfers)

### Phase 2: Exit South Africa (Month 3)

1. **Cease South African tax residency:**
   - Formal notification to SARS
   - Physical departure (or clear evidence of intent)
   - Terminate SA bank accounts (or convert to non-resident)
   - Cancel medical aid, driver's license, etc.

2. **Exchange control:**
   - Up to R11 million per individual can be transferred offshore annually without FIA approval
   - For larger amounts: FIA application (60-90 days)
   - Consider phased transfers

3. **Final SA tax return:**
   - Declare all crypto disposals up to departure
   - Claim CGT annual exclusion (R40,000)
   - Ensure no outstanding SARS obligations

### Phase 3: Paraguay Residency (Month 4-6)

1. **Submit application:**
   - Documents to Migraciones
   - First trip for biometrics (3-5 days)
   - Receive provisional approval

2. **Set up banking:**
   - Open Ueno account
   - Register with X4T
   - Apply for Schwab International (backup)

3. **Receive cedula:**
   - Final approval (30-60 days)
   - Cedula de Identidad issued
   - RUC (tax ID) obtained

### Phase 4: Post-Residency (Month 6+)

1. **Transfer crypto to Paraguay:**
   - Move holdings through X4T or direct custody
   - Maintain records for DNIT Res 47 filing

2. **File DNIT declaration:**
   - Annual reporting (not a tax)
   - Local accountant: ~$200-$500/year

3. **Apply for citizenship:**
   - Clock starts from cedula date
   - Eligible after 3 years

## Case Study: South African Crypto Trader

**Profile:** Active DeFi trader, R5M portfolio, R2M annual gains, classified as trader by SARS

### South Africa Scenario
- R2,000,000 gain x 45% = R900,000 tax
- Plus wealth effect (high cost of living in SA)
- SARS monitoring via CARF
- **Total tax: ~R900,000/year**

### Paraguay Scenario
- R2,000,000 gain x 0% = R0 tax
- DNIT annual filing: ~R3,000 ($200) accountant fee
- No CRS — no automatic reporting to SARS
- **Total tax: R0/year**

**Annual savings: R900,000**

## Common Challenges for South Africans

### Challenge 1: Exchange Control

South Africa's Reserve Bank controls cross-border capital movements. The annual single discretionary allowance is R1 million, with a R11 million foreign investment allowance (requires tax clearance).

**Solution:** Work with a forex specialist. Plan transfers over multiple months. Obtain FIA approval for larger amounts.

### Challenge 2: SARS Crypto Scrutiny

SARS has become significantly more aggressive on crypto. The CARF framework (Crypto-Asset Reporting Framework) will give SARS direct visibility into foreign exchange accounts.

**Solution:** Be fully compliant on your exit. Declare everything. Leave clean.

### Challenge 3: Spanish Language

Paraguay's official languages are Spanish and Guarani. English is not widely spoken outside expat circles.

**Solution:** Start learning Spanish now. Basic conversational level is sufficient for residency and citizenship.

## The Bottom Line

South Africa's crypto tax regime is punishing — up to 45% for traders, 18% effective for investors, with SARS enforcement intensifying. Paraguay offers 0% on foreign crypto gains, no CRS, and a clear path to a second passport.

The document complexity that makes South African exits difficult also creates a natural moat. Those who navigate the paperwork successfully benefit from a regime that hasn't been overrun by expats. Yet.

The steps are clear, the math is overwhelming, and the window won't stay open forever. Start your document preparation today.

slug: "paraguay-stappenplan-residency"

author: "Nexa Paraguay"

language: "nl"
excerpt: "Concreet 6-maanden stappenplan voor Nederlandse crypto-investeerders die Box 3 willen ontvluchten en residency in Paraguay willen verkrijgen. Maand 1: documenten verzamelen. Maand 2: eerste reis. Maand 3-4: verwerking. Maand 5: tweede reis. Maand 6: cedula. Kosten per stap inclusief."

## Stappenplan: Van Box 3 naar Paraguay in 6 Maanden

De Nederlandse Belastingdienst gaat vanaf 2026 daadwerkelijk 36% belasting heffen op ongerealiseerde cryptowinsten onder het nieuwe Box 3-stelsel. Voor serieuse crypto-investeerders is dit een ramp.

Paraguay biedt een uitweg: 0% belasting op buitenlandse crypto-inkomsten, geen vermogensbelasting, geen CRS-uitwisseling, en een snelle route naar een tweede paspoort.

Dit stappenplan laat zien hoe je in 6 maanden van Box 3 naar een Paraguayaanse cedula gaat.

## Overzicht: De 6 Maanden

| Maand | Activiteit | Locatie | Geschatte Kosten |
|-------|-----------|---------|------------------|
| Maand 1 | Documenten verzamelen & legaliseren | Nederland | €400-€800 |
| Maand 2 | Eerste reis naar Paraguay | Paraguay | €1.500-€2.500 |
| Maand 3-4 | Verwerking aanvraag | Nederland (wachten) | €200 (vertalingen) |
| Maand 5 | Tweede reis & biometrie | Paraguay | €1.500-€2.500 |
| Maand 6 | Cedula ophalen & banken openen | Paraguay | €500-€1.000 |
| **Totaal** | | | **€4.100-€7.000** |

## Maand 1: Documenten Verzamelen (Nederland)

De meeste tijd gaat zitten in het correct krijgen van de documenten. Begin direct in maand 1.

### Wat je nodig hebt

**1. Paspoort (kopie)**
- Laat een kopie notarieel waarmerken
- Let op: paspoort moet nog minimaal 6 maanden geldig zijn

**2. Geboorteakte**
- Vraag een internationaal uittreksel aan bij de gemeente waar je geboren bent
- Kosten: ongeveer €25-€50
- Laat deze vervolgens legaliseren met een apostille

**3. Verklaring Omtrent het Gedrag (VOG)**
- VOG voor naturalisatie/buitenland aanvragen via Justis
- Kosten: €41,65
- Let op: vraag de internationaal bruikbare variant
- Laat ook deze voorzien van een apostille

**4. Huwelijksakte (indien van toepassing)**
- Internationaal uittreksel van de burgerlijke stand
- Apstille laten zetten

**5. Bankreferentie**
- Vraag een referentiebrief bij je Nederlandse bank
- Deze moet je banksaldo en rekeninghistorie aantonen
- Laat deze vertalen naar het Spaans

**6. Bewijs van crypto-bezit**
- Uitdraaien van exchange-transacties (Binance, Coinbase, Bitvavo, etc.)
- Wallet-overzichten
- Dit dient als bewijs van inkomsten/middelen voor de immigratiedienst
- Let op: Paraguay is flexibel — crypto-bezit wordt geaccepteerd

**7. Medische verklaring**
- Van een huisarts of specialist (niet ouder dan 3 maanden)
- Verklaring dat je in goede gezondheid verkeert
- Apstille nodig? Meestal niet, maar check met je advocaat

### Kosten Maand 1

| Item | Kosten |
|------|--------|
| Geboorteakte + apostille | €50-€100 |
| VOG + apostille | €80-€150 |
| Notariskosten (kopieën waarmerken) | €50-€100 |
| Huwelijksakte (indien nodig) | €50-€100 |
| Totaal | €230-€450 |

## Maand 2: Eerste Reis naar Paraguay

Je eerste bezoek aan Paraguay is cruciaal. Je moet persoonlijk verschijnen voor de immigratiedienst.

### Voor de reis

**Schakel een Paraguayaanse advocaat in**
- Dit is verplicht voor de residency-aanvraag
- Kosten: €1.500-€2.500 voor het volledige traject
- Kies een gespecialiseerde immigratierecht advocaat
- Vraag referenties van andere expats

### Wat je in Paraguay doet (5-7 dagen)

**Dag 1-2: Aankomst en oriëntatie**
- Vlieg naar Silvio Pettirossi Airport (ASU) in Asuncion
- Check in bij een hotel of Airbnb (€30-€70/nacht)
- Ontmoet je advocaat

**Dag 3: Documenten indienen**
- Bezoek Migraciones (Dirección General de Migraciones)
- Dien alle originele documenten in (met Spaanse vertaling)
- Betaal de aanvraagkosten (€200-€350)

**Dag 4-5: Medisch onderzoek**
- Bezoek een lokale arts voor het medisch onderzoek
- Dit is een formaliteit, kost ongeveer €40-€60
- Eventueel: vingerafdrukken voor de Cedula

**Dag 6-7: Vrije tijd / verkenning**
- Verken Asuncion of Ciudad del Este
- Open alvast een bankrekening bij Ueno (kan ook online)
- Maak kennis met de lokale expat community

### Tips voor je eerste reis

- **Spaans:** Basiskennis is voldoende, maar een tolk kan helpen
- **Contant geld:** Neem voldoende contant mee (€500+) voor lokale betalingen
- **Veiligheid:** Asuncion is veilig in de juiste wijken (Villa Morra, Carmelitas)
- **Internet:** 50-300 Mbps fiber beschikbaar, koop een lokale SIM (Tigo, Personal)

### Kosten Maand 2

| Item | Kosten |
|------|--------|
| Vlucht (retour, Nederland-Asuncion) | €800-€1.200 |
| Verblijf (7 nachten) | €210-€490 |
| Advocaat (voorschot) | €500-€1.000 |
| Aanvraagkosten Migraciones | €200-€350 |
| Medisch onderzoek | €40-€60 |
| Lokale kosten (eten, vervoer) | €150-€300 |
| Totaal | €1.900-€3.400 |

## Maand 3-4: Verwerkingstijd (Nederland)

Je bent terug in Nederland. Nu begint het wachten.

### Wat gebeurt er in Paraguay

- Migraciones verwerkt je aanvraag
- De achtergrondcheck wordt uitgevoerd
- Dit duurt 30-60 dagen

### Wat jij in Nederland doet

**1. Huis opzeggen / verhuizen regelen**
- Huurovereenkomst opzeggen
- Of woning aanhouden als tweede verblijf (let op fiscale gevolgen)

**2. Bankzaken regelen**
- Nederlandse bankrekening omzetten naar niet-ingezetene (indien mogelijk)
- Cryptogelden ordenen voor verhuizing
- Grote posities in stabielere assets zetten

**3. Fiscale exit plannen**
- Overleg met een fiscaal jurist over het beëindigen van Nederlandse belastingplicht
- Laatste aangifte in Box 3 voorbereiden
- Let op: geen exit tax in Nederland op crypto (moment van vertrek is belastbaar moment in Box 3)

**4. Spaanse les**
- Begin met Spaans leren (Duolingo, Italki, taalschool)
- Basisniveau is nodig voor het naturalisatiegesprek over 3 jaar
- Ook handig voor dagelijks leven in Paraguay

### Kosten Maand 3-4

| Item | Kosten |
|------|--------|
| Spaanse les (online, 2 maanden) | €100-€300 |
| Fiscaal advies | €500-€1.000 |
| Diverse kosten | €100-€200 |
| Totaal | €700-€1.500 |

## Maand 5: Tweede Reis naar Paraguay

Je aanvraag is goedgekeurd. Nu moet je terug naar Paraguay voor de definitieve stappen.

### Wat je doet (3-5 dagen)

**1. Biometrie en foto**
- Vingerafdrukken voor de Cedula de Identidad
- Pasfoto (4x4 cm, witte achtergrond)

**2. Cedula ophalen**
- Je ontvangt je officiële Paraguayaanse identiteitskaart
- Dit is je bewijs van legaal verblijf

**3. RUC aanvragen (belastingnummer)**
- Registratie bij DNIT voor je belastingnummer
- Dit is nodig voor bankrekeningen en de jaarlijkse DNIT-aangifte

**4. Bankrekeningen openen**
- Ueno: digitale bank, crypto-vriendelijk (€0-€50)
- X4T account: crypto exchange (€0)
- Eventueel: Itaú of Banco Continental voor traditioneel bankieren

**5. Oobit activeren**
- Crypto spending app voor dagelijks gebruik
- Koppel je wallet en begin met uitgeven

### Kosten Maand 5

| Item | Kosten |
|------|--------|
| Vlucht (retour) | €800-€1.200 |
| Verblijf (5 nachten) | €150-€350 |
| Cedula-kosten | €50-€100 |
| RUC-registratie (via accountant) | €50-€100 |
| Advocaat (slotbetaling) | €500-€1.000 |
| Lokale kosten | €100-€200 |
| Totaal | €1.650-€2.950 |

## Maand 6: Definitieve Vestiging

Je bent nu legaal ingezetene van Paraguay. Tijd voor de laatste stappen.

### Finaliseren

**1. Verhuizing afronden**
- Laatste spullen overbrengen of opslaan in Nederland
- Adreswijzigingen doorgeven

**2. Fiscale formaliteiten**
- DNIT Res 47/2026: jaarlijkse crypto-aangifte regelen via lokale accountant (€200-€500/jaar)
- Dit is géén belasting — alleen een meldingsplicht

**3. Bankinfrastructuur**
- Ueno daily banking actief
- X4T voor crypto-handel
- Oobit voor crypto-uitgaven
- Overweeg Schwab International voor US-markttoegang

**4. Nederlandse zaken afsluiten**
- Laatste aangifte inkomstenbelasting indienen
- Zorgverzekering opzeggen
- AOW-opbouw checken (vrijwillige voortzetting mogelijk)

### Kosten Maand 6

| Item | Kosten |
|------|--------|
| Accountant (DNIT filing) | €200-€500 |
| Verhuizing / opslag | €500-€2.000 |
| Diverse kosten | €200-€500 |
| Totaal | €900-€3.000 |

## Totale Kostenoverzicht

| Fase | Minimale Kosten | Maximale Kosten |
|------|----------------|-----------------|
| Maand 1: Documenten | €230 | €450 |
| Maand 2: Eerste reis | €1.900 | €3.400 |
| Maand 3-4: Verwerking | €700 | €1.500 |
| Maand 5: Tweede reis | €1.650 | €2.950 |

## Veelgestelde Vragen

### Moet ik permanent in Paraguay blijven wonen?

Nee. Er is geen minimale fysieke aanwezigheidsplicht. Je moet wel eens in de 3 jaar terugkeren om je cedula te verlengen.

### Worden mijn crypto-bezittingen belast in Paraguay?

Nee. Paraguay heeft een territoriaal belastingstelsel. Crypto-winsten uit buitenlandse bronnen worden belast tegen 0%.

### Wat kost een advocaat?

€1.500-€2.500 voor het volledige residency-traject. Dit is de belangrijkste investering — kies een goede.

### Is DNIT Res 47/2026 een cryptobelasting?

Nee. Het is een meldingsplicht (AML/KYC). Je rapporteert je crypto-bezit boven $5.000, maar betaalt 0% belasting.

### Kan ik mijn Nederlandse paspoort houden?

Ja. Paraguay staat dubbele nationaliteit volledig toe. Je kunt zowel Nederlands als Paraguayaans staatsburger zijn.

### Hoe lang duurt het voor ik Paraguayaans staatsburger ben?

3 jaar vanaf de datum dat je cedula is afgegeven. Geen aaneengesloten verblijf vereist.

## De Bottom Line

Het 6-maanden stappenplan van Box 3 naar Paraguay is haalbaar voor elke serieuze crypto-investeerder. De kosten (€5.000-€11.000) zijn een fractie van wat je jaarlijks aan Nederlandse belasting bespaart.

De documenten zijn het meeste werk. De advocaat is de belangrijkste investering. De reizen naar Paraguay zijn aangenaam. En het resultaat — 0% belasting op cryptowinsten, geen vermogensbelasting, een tweede paspoort binnen 3 jaar — is het meer dan waard.

De tijd om te handelen is nu. Hoe langer je wacht, hoe meer Box 3 je betaalt en hoe dichter de Paraguayaanse residency-deuren mogelijk sluiten.
